item.title: Short and medium-term outcomes of ustekinumab biosimilar in patients with Crohn's disease in naïve patients and after switching from originator.

item.title: Analysis of possible baseline and treatment-course factors associated with non-remission in patients with Crohn's disease treated with ustekinumab: a retrospective real-life analysis.

item.description: Clinical pharmacology and therapeutics - The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) used an Active Postmarket Risk Identification and Analysis (ARIA) system to address a safety issue (serious infection) identified during clinical review ...

item.title: Performance evaluation of an adalimumab biosimilar autoinjector and its reference device: comparative use human factors study in patients with rheumatoid arthritis and juvenile idiopathic arthritis.

item.title: Multiomic characterisation of the clinical efficacy of guselkumab induction therapy in ulcerative colitis.

item.title: Lower drug clearance of adalimumab is associated with proactive therapeutic drug monitoring and mucosal healing in patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - There are limited data regarding adalimumab (ADM) clearance in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). The aim of this study was to identify factors associated with ADM clearance and to assess its associ...

item.title: Additive effects of fecal microbiota transplantation and infliximab on gut microbiome and metabolome in refractory inflammatory bowel disease patients.

item.title: Advancing drug discovery for Inflammatory bowel diseases through human intestinal organoid-based models.
item.description: Expert opinion on drug discovery - Review - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD), including Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, are chronic inflammatory conditions that affect millions of patients worldwide. Despite recent advances, the ava...

item.title: Orally deliverable Perilla frutescens-derived nanovesicles as natural bioactive nanocarriers for colon-targeted colitis therapy via microenvironment reprogramming.

item.title: Exploring the mechanism of Qinlian Yuyang Decoction in the treatment of ulcerative colitis based on multi-omics technology.
item.description: Chinese medicine - The global incidence of ulcerative colitis (UC) is increasing, yet effective clinical treatment options remain limited. Qinlian Yuyang Decoction (QYD), a modified formulation based on Gegen Qinlian...

item.title: Crohn`s disease, vedolizumab and autoinmmune hemolytic anemia: coincidence or causality?

item.title: Recovery of response and long-term outcomes following loss of response and dose escalation of subcutaneous infliximab: a post hoc analysis of the LIBERTY-CD & LIBERTY-UC trials.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Rapidity of onset of efficacy following dose escalation of subcutaneous infliximab after loss of response remains unclear in Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC). This post hoc analysis...

item.title: Red-light-activated living bacterial electron generator for on-demand drug release in colonic inflammation.
item.description: Journal of controlled release : official journal of the Controlled Release Society - Inflammatory bowel disease demands spatiotemporally precise drug delivery, yet the variable gut redox environment limits stimuli-responsive nanocarriers. item.title: The decline of 6-thioguanine nucleotides is not linked to impaired efficacy or safety of thiopurines in pregnant women with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: British journal of clinical pharmacology - Thiopurines are used to maintain remission in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). These drugs are metabolized into 6-thioguanine nucleotides (6-TGN), associated with efficacy, and 6-methylmercaptopur...

item.title: Comparing the pharmacokinetics of adalimumab originator and biosimilar product in patients with Inflammatory bowel disease or autoimmune disease.
item.description: Saudi pharmaceutical journal : SPJ : the official publication of the Saudi Pharmaceutical Society - Adalimumab is a monoclonal antibody approved for managing inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and other autoimmune conditions. item.title: First-Line Guselkumab Combined with Exclusive Enteral Nutrition in Biologic-Naïve Young‑Onset Crohn's Disease: A Three-Case Series from China.

item.title: Colonic biopsy-associated microbial signatures are predictive of response to anti-TNFα biological therapy in Crohn's disease.
item.description: Frontiers in cellular and infection microbiology - Crohn's disease (CD) is commonly treated with biologic therapies, including anti-TNFα agents, vedolizumab (VDZ), and ustekinumab (USTE), yet only a subset of patients respond to these treatments. T...
